title: Augmented reality planning and viewing of dental treatment outcomes
abstract: A processing device generates a visual overlay comprising a virtual model of a dental arch that represents a treatment outcome for the dental arch. The processing device outputs the visual overlay to an augmented reality (AR) display or virtual reality (VR) display. The processing device receives user input based on a user interaction with the virtual model of the dental arch, wherein the user input modifies the virtual model of the dental arch and determines an update to the treatment outcome for the dental arch based on a user input. The processing device generates an updated treatment plan based on the update to the treatment outcome and updates the visual overlay, wherein the updated visual overlay comprises a view of the dental arch after the updated treatment outcome.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A system comprising: a memory device; and a processing device operatively coupled to the memoiy device, the processing device to: generate a visual overlay comprising a virtual model of a dental arch that represents a treatment outcome for the dental arch; output the visual overlay to an augmented reality (AR) display or virtual reality (VR) display; receive user input based on a user interaction with the virtual model of the dental arch, wherein the user input modifies the virtual model of the dental arch; determine an update to the treatment outcome for the dental arch based on the user input; generate an updated treatment plan based on the update to the treatment outcome; and update the visual overlay, wherein the updated visual overlay comprises a view of the dental arch after the updated treatm ent outcome. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the processing device is further to: determine a first midline of the dental arch after the treatment outcome; receive image data of a face from an image capture device associated with the AR di splay; and process the image data to determine a position of a second midline of the face, wherein the vi sual overlay further comprises an indication of the second midline of the face and an indication of the first midl ine of the dental arch. 3. The system of claim 2, wherein the user input comprises an update to the midline of the treatment outcome, and wherein the processing device is further to update the first midline and the second midline based on the updated treatment plan. 4. The system of claim 1, wherein the virtual model is a virtual three-dimensional (3D) model, and wherein to receive the user input, the processing device is to: receive image data from an image capture device associated with the AR di splay or the VR displ ay; identify an object in the image data; and determine the update to the treatment outcome based on an interaction of the object with the virtual 3D model of the dental arch. 5. The system of claim 4, wherein the processing device is further to: determine that the update to the treatment outcome violates a treatment outcome rule; and output an indication that the treatment outcome violates the treatment outcome rule. 6. The system of claim 5, wherein to output the indication that the treatment outcome violates the treatment outcome rule, the processi ng device is further to cause the object in the image data to provide haptic feedback to a user. 7. The system of claim 4, wherein to determine the update to the treatment outcome, the processing device is further to: determine that the object i s in a position corresponding to a virtual position of an element of the virtual 3D model ; determine a movement of the object relative to the element; and generate an update to the treatment outcome compri sing an update to the position of the element corresponding to the movement of the object relative to the element. 8. The system of claim 4, herein the processing device is further to: provide a first level of haptic feedback in response to the object interacting with the virtual 3D model of the dental arch; and provide a second level of haptic feedback in response to a movement of an update to the treatment outcome satisfying a threshold. 9. The system of claim 1, wherein to output the visual overlay to the AR display or the VR display, the processing device is to: receive image data of a face from an image capture device associated with the AR display; detect a position of the dental arch in the image data; and output the visual overlay to a position on the AR display to superimpose the visual ov erlay over a view of the dental arch on the AR di splay. 10. The system of claim 1, wherein the AR display comprises AR eyewear or AR headwear and wherein outputting the visual ov erlay to the AR display comprises projecting the visual overlay onto the AR eyewear or the AR headwear, and wherein the VR display comprises VR eyewear or VR headwear and wherein outputting the visual ov erlay to the VR display comprises projecting the visual overlay onto the VR eyewear or the VR headwear. 1 1 . A method comprising: generating a visual overlay comprising a v irtual model of a dental arch that represents a treatment outcome for the dental arch; outputting the visual ov erlay to an augmented reality ( AR) display or virtual reality (VR) display; receiv ing user input based on a user interaction with the virtual model of the dental arch, wherein the user input modifies the virtual model of the dental arch; determining an update to the treatment outcome for the dental arch based on the user input; generating an updated treatment plan based on the update to the treatment outcome; and updating the visual overlay, wherein the updated visual overlay comprises a view of the dental arch after the updated treatment outcome. 12. The method of claim 1 1, further comprising: determining a first midline of the dental arch after the treatment outcome; receiv ing image data of a face from an image capture device associated with the AR display; and processing the image data to determine a position of a second midline of the face. 

 wherein the vi sual overlay further comprises an indication of the second midline of the face and an indication of the first midline of the dental arch. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the user input comprises an update to the midline of the treatment outcome, the method further compri sing: updating the first midline and the second midline based on the updated treatment plan. 14. The method of claim 1 1 , wherein the virtual model is a virtual three-dimensional (3D) model, the method further compri sing: receiving image data from an image capture device associated with the AR display or the VR display; identifying an object in the image data; and determining the update to the treatment outcome based on an interaction of the object with the virtual 3D model of the dental arch. 15. The method of claim 14, the method further comprising: determining that the update to the treatment outcome violates a treatment outcome rule; and outputting an indication that the treatment outcome violates the treatment outcome rule. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein outputting the indication that the treatment outcome violates the treatment outcome rule compri ses causing the object in the image data to provide haptic feedback to a user. 1 7. The method of claim 14, wherein determining the update to the treatment outcome comprises: determining that the object is in a position corresponding to a virtual position of an element of the virtual 3D model ; determining a movement of the object relative to the element; and generating an update to the treatment outcome comprising an update to the position of the element corresponding to the movement of the object relative to the element. 18. The method of claim 14, further comprising: providing a first level of haptic feedback in response to the object interacting with the virtual 3D model of the dental arch ; and providing a second level of haptic feedback in response to a movement of an update to the treatment outcome satisfying a threshold. 19. The method of claim 1 1, wherein outputting the visual overlay to the AR display or the VR display comprises: receiving image data of a face from an image capture device associated with the AR display; detecting a position of the dental arch in the image data; and outputting the visual overlay to a position on the AR display to superimpose the vi sual overlay over a view of the dental arch on the AR display. 20. The method of claim 1 1, wherein the AR display comprises AR eyewear or AR headwear and wherein outputting the vi sual overlay to the AR display compri ses projecting the visual overlay onto the AR eyewear or the AR headwear, and wherein the VR display compri ses VR eyewear or VR headwear and wherein outputting the visual overlay to the VR display comprises projecting the visual overlay onto the VR eyewear or the VR headwear. 2 1 . A method comprising: receiving image data of a face from an image capture device associated with an augmented reality (AR) display; processing, by a processing device, the image data to a) identify a mouth in the image data, b) identify a dental arch in the mouth, and c) determine a position of the dental arch relative to a position of the AR display; determining, by the processing device, a treatment outcome for the dental arch; generating, by the processing device, a visual overlay comprising an indication of the treatment outcome at the determined position of the dental arch; and outputting the visual overlay to the AR display, wherein the visual overlay is superimposed over a view of the dental arch on the AR display. 22. The method of claim 21 , further comprising; tracking the position of the dental arch, a shape of the mouth, and exposed portions of the dental arch; and updating the visual overlay in response to an update to at least one of the position of the dental arch, the shape of the mouth or the exposed portions of the dental arch. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein tracking the position of the dental arch comprises: determining an offset vector from the AR display to the position of the dental arch; identifying a change in the position of the AR di splay; and updating the position of the dental arch in response to the change in the position of the AR display. 24. The method of claim 2 1 , wherein the AR display comprises AR eyewear or AR headwear and herein outputting the visual overl ay compri ses projecting the visual overlay onto the AR eyewear or the AR headwear. 25. The method of claim 2 1 , wherein outputting the visual overlay to the AR di splay comprises: capturing a live video feed of the face; and superimposing the treatment outcome over the view of the dental arch in each frame of the live video feed. 26. The method of claim 2 1 , wherein determining the treatment outcome for the dental arch compri ses: generating a virtual three-dimensional model of the dental arch that shows the dental arch after application of orthodontic treatment; determining a perspectiv e of the A R di play relative to the position of the dental arch; determining a portion of the virtual three-dimensional model that would be v isible from the perspective of the AR display, and generating a two dimensional perspective of the portion of the virtual three- dimensional model that would be visible from the perspective of the AR display. 27. The method of claim 21 , wherein the AR display is worn by a user that looks into a mirror, and wherein the image data is a reflection of the face of the user as shown in the mirror. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein determining the treatment outcome comprises selecting a generic idealized dental arch. 29. The method of claim 27, wherein the image data comprises an image of the AR display, wherein the arch comprises an upper arch and a lower arch, and wherein processing the image data comprises: determining a fixed distance between the upper arch of the user and the image capture device based on the image data; and determining an area in the image data to search for the lower arch based on a) the fixed distance between the upper arch and the image capture device and b) a modeled range of motion for the lower arch relative to the upper arch. 30. The method of claim 2 1 , wherein the AR display comprises a kiosk. 31 . The method of claim 21, wherein the AR display comprises a mobile computing device. 32. The method of claim 2 1 , wherein the dental arch compri ses markers placed on one or more teeth of the dental arch, and wherein identifying the dental arch in the mouth comprises identifying locations of the markers in the image data. 33. A method comprising: receiving image data from an image capture device associated with an augmented reality (AR ) display; processing, by a processing device, the image data to identify a patient identi ier in the image data; determining, by the processing device, a medical record associated with the patient identifier; retrieving dental data included in the medical record; and outputting the dental data to the AR display. 34. The method of claim 33, wherein the patient identifier comprises a one-dimensional barcode or a two-dimensional barcode. 35. The method of claim 33, wherein the patient identifier comprises an image. 36. The method of claim 35, wherein the image is an image of a face of a patient associated with the medical record, and wherein processing the image data comprises: performing facial identification to identify a face in the image data; and performing facial recognition to recognize the face in the image data as being the face of the patient. 37. The method of claim 33, wherein the dental data comprises a virtual three- dimensional (3D) model of a dental arch, the method further comprising: generating a rendering of the virtual 3 D model, wherein the dental data output to the AR display comprises the rendering. 38. The method of claim 37, further comprising: detecting an object in the image data that sati sfies a mapping criterion; determining at least one of a first position or a first orientation of the object in the image data; setting at least one of a second position or a second orientation of the rendering of the virtual 3D model based on the at least one of the first position or the first orientation; detecting a change in at least one of the first position or the first orientation; and adj usting at least one of the second position or the second orientation based on the change in at least one of the first position or the first orientation. 39. The method of claim 38, wherein detecting the object in the image data that satisfies a mapping criterion comprises: determining that the object comprises the patient identifier. 40. The method of claim 38, wherein detecting the object in the image data that satisfies a mapping criterion comprises: performing image processing to identify a hand in the image data; and performing image processing to identify that the object is held in the hand. 41. The method of claim 38, wherein detecting the obj ect in the image data that satisfies a mapping criterion comprises: determining that the object has a first geometrical configuration that matches a second geometrical configuration of a designated input device for manipulation of the virtual 3D model. 42. The method of claim 37, wherein the virtual 3D model of the dental arch represents a treatment outcome for the dental arch, the method further comprising: receiving user input based on a user interaction with the virtual 3D model of the dental arch, wherein the user input modifies the virtual 3D model of the dental arch; determining an update to the treatment outcome for the dental arch based on the user input; generating an updated treatment plan based on the update to the treatment outcome; and updating a representation of the virtual 3D model, wherein the updated representation comprises a view of the dental arch after the updated treatment outcome. 43. The method of claim 33, wherein the AR display comprises AR eyewear or A R headwear and wherein outputting the dental data comprises projecting a visual overlay comprising a representati on of the dental data onto the AR eyewear or the AR headwear. 44. A system comprising: a memory device; and a processing device operatively coupled to the memory device, the processing device to: receive image data from an image capture device associated with an augmented reality ( AR) display; process the image data to identify a patient identifier in the image data; determine a medical record associated with the patient identifier; retrieve dental data included in the medical record; and output the dental data to the AR display. 45. The system of claim 44, wherein the patient identifier compri ses a one-dimensional barcode or a two-dimensional barcode. 46. The system of claim 44, wherein the patient identifier comprises an image. 47. The system of claim 44, wherein the image i s an image of a face of a patient associated with the medical record, and wherein processing the image data comprises: performing facial identification to identify a face in the image data; and performing facial recognition to recognize the face in the image data as being the face of the patient. 48. The system of claim 44, wherein the dental data comprises a virtual three-dimensional (3D) model of a dental arch, and herein the processing device is further to: generate a rendering of the virtual 3D model, wherein the dental data output to the AR di splay comprises the rendering. 49. The system of claim 48, wherein the processing device is further to: detect an object in the image data that satisfies a mapping criterion; determine at least one of a first position or a first orientation of the object i n the image data; set at least one of a second position or a second ori entation of the rendering of the virtual 3D model based on the at least one of the first position or the first orientation; detect a change in at least one of the first position or the first orientation; and adj ust at least one of the second position or the second orientation based on the change in at least one of the first position or the first orientation . 50. The system of claim 49, wherein detecting the object in the image data that satisfies a mapping criterion comprises: determining that the object comprises the patient identifier. 51 . The system of claim 49, wherein detecting the object in the image data that satisfies a mapping criterion comprises: performing image processing to identify a hand in the image data; and performing image processing to identify that the object i s held in the hand. 52. The system of claim 49, wherein detecting the object in the image data that satisfies a mapping criterion comprises: 

 determining that the object has a first geometrical configuration that matches a second geometrical configuration of a designated input device for manipulation of the virtual 3D model. 53. The system of claim 48, wherein the virtual 3D model of the dental arch represents a treatment outcome for the dental arch, and wherein the processing device is further to: receive user input based on a user interaction with the virtual 3D model of the dental arch, wherein the user input modifies the virtual 3D model of the dental arch; determine an update to the treatment outcome for the dental arch based on the user input; generate an updated treatment plan based on the update to the treatment outcome; and update a representation of the virtual D model, wherein the updated representation comprises a view of the dental arch after the updated treatment outcome. 54. The system of claim 44, wherein the AR display comprises AR eyewear or AR headvvear and herein outputting the dental data comprises projecting a vi sual overlay comprising a representation of the dental data onto the AR eyewear or the AR headvvear.
